name: Chigorin Chess
author: AGP
description: Chigorin Chess (Ralph Betza, 2002) — a Knights army against a Bishops army on the standard 8×8 board, named for Mikhail Chigorin's supposed preference for Knights. White fields four Knights and a Chancellor (Rook+Knight) in place of the Bishops and Queen; Black plays four Bishops and an orthodox Queen in place of the Knights. All other FIDE rules apply — castling, en passant, checkmate, and the usual draws — and each side's Pawns promote only to pieces of its own army (White: Chancellor/Rook/Knight; Black: Queen/Rook/Bishop). Matches the Fairy-Stockfish 'chigorin' ruleset; covered in Abstract Games issue 24.
